ucs.title: [LLM] Moses Staff (Marigold Sandstorm) ProxyShell-to-DCSrv chain: IIS worker spawning discovery + SMB lateral movement
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical Moses Staff initial-access chain: exploitation of an unpatched on-prem Exchange (ProxyShell / ProxyLogon) where w3wp.exe spawns a webshell (China Chopper-style) that then runs net.exe/systeminfo/nltest discovery and pivots over SMB with PsExec/WMI - the exact tradecraft documented by Check Point and FortiGuard against Israeli targets in 2021-2023.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_name) as child_procs dc(Processes.process_name) as distinct_children from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="UMWorkerProcess.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe","whoami.exe","nltest.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","hostname.exe","quser.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe","psexec.exe","wmic.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process Processes.user _time span=1h | where distinct_children >= 3 | rename Processes.* as * | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let exchange_servers = DeviceProcessEvents | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe" and InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("MSExchange","OWA","Autodiscover","ECP") | distinct DeviceId;
let recon = DeviceProcessEvents
| where DeviceId in (exchange_servers)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| where FileName in~ ("net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe","whoami.exe","nltest.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","hostname.exe","quser.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe")
| summarize ReconCmds=make_set(ProcessCommandLine,20), ReconBins=dcount(FileName), FirstSeen=min(Timestamp), LastSeen=max(Timestamp) by DeviceId, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName
| where ReconBins >= 3;
let pivot = DeviceProcessEvents
| where DeviceId in (exchange_servers)
| where (FileName in~ ("psexec.exe","paexec.exe","wmic.exe")) or (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("\\admin$","\\c$","\\ipc$","-accepteula","process call create"))
| summarize PivotCmds=make_set(ProcessCommandLine,10), PivotTime=min(Timestamp) by DeviceId, DeviceName;
recon | join kind=inner pivot on DeviceId
| where PivotTime between (FirstSeen .. (LastSeen + 6h))
| project DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, FirstSeen, LastSeen, ReconCmds, PivotCmds
ucs.rationale: Moses Staff's tradecraft is overwhelmingly Exchange-borne: Check Point's Nov-2021 and FortiGuard's 2022 reports both describe ProxyShell webshells dropping discovery output and then PsExec/WMI lateral movement to seed DCSrv. The signature is w3wp.exe (an IIS worker - never a legitimate parent for net.exe/nltest/PsExec) immediately followed by SMB pivoting on the same Exchange server. This is far n

ucs.title: [LLM] Moses Staff DCSrv destructive encryptor deployment (PyDCrypt + DiskCryptor dcrypt.sys service)
ucs.description: Hunts the actor-unique terminal payload: Moses Staff's DCSrv wiper-encryptor (a militarized fork of the open-source DiskCryptor) installed as a Windows service named 'DCSrv' with the dcrypt.sys driver, often launched by the PyInstaller-bundled PyDCrypt loader. There is no legitimate enterprise use of DiskCryptor on a server, so a hit is near-certain Moses Staff / Marigold Sandstorm.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmd values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("PyDCrypt.exe","pydcrypt.exe","dcsrv.exe","DCSrv.exe","dcinst.exe") OR Processes.process IN ("*sc*create*DCSrv*","*sc*\\\\*create*DCSrv*","*dcrypt.sys*","*-install*-pwd*","*-encrypt*-pwd*")) by Processes.dest Processes.process_name _time span=10m | append [| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as path values(Filesystem.file_name) as fname from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name IN ("dcrypt.sys","dcapi.dll","dcrypt.exe","DCSrv.exe","PyDCrypt.exe") OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\System32\\drivers\\dcrypt.sys") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_name _time span=10m] | append [| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Changes.object) as regkey values(All_Changes.object_path) as regpath from datamodel=Change.All_Changes where All_Changes.object_path="*\\Services\\DCSrv*" OR All_Changes.object_path="*\\Services\\dcrypt*" by All_Changes.dest _time span=10m] | stats values(*) as * count by dest | where count >= 2 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
let dcsrv_proc = DeviceProcessEvents
| where (FileName in~ ("PyDCrypt.exe","DCSrv.exe","dcsrv.exe","dcinst.exe","dcrypt.exe"))
   or (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("sc create DCSrv","sc.exe create DCSrv","DCSrv","dcrypt.sys") and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("create","start","binPath","-encrypt","-install"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, SHA256;
let diskcryptor_drv = DeviceFileEvents
| where FileName =~ "dcrypt.sys" or FolderPath endswith "\\System32\\drivers\\dcrypt.sys" or FileName in~ ("dcapi.dll","dccon.exe","dcrypt.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, ActionType, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
let dcsrv_svc = DeviceRegistryEvents
| where RegistryKey has_any ("\\Services\\DCSrv","\\Services\\dcrypt")
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData, ActionType, InitiatingProcessFileName;
let driver_load = DeviceEvents
| where ActionType in ("DriverLoad","KernelModuleLoad")
| where FileName =~ "dcrypt.sys" or FolderPath has "dcrypt.sys"
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, InitiatingProcessFileName;
union dcsrv_proc, diskcryptor_drv, dcsrv_svc, driver_load
| summarize Hits=count(), Signals=make_set(strcat(FileName,"|",coalesce(ProcessCommandLine, FolderPath, RegistryKey, "")), 30), FirstSeen=min(Timestamp), LastSeen=max(Timestamp) by DeviceId, DeviceName
| where Hits >= 1
| order by FirstSeen asc
ucs.rationale: DCSrv is a Moses Staff-exclusive payload (Check Point CPR Nov-2021, Microsoft DEV-0500/Marigold Sandstorm reporting). The combination of (a) a service literally named 'DCSrv', (b) the DiskCryptor driver dcrypt.sys / dcapi.dll, and (c) PyDCrypt.exe (their PyInstaller-bundled launcher) is not seen in legitimate enterprise software and not reused by other ransomware crews - unlike LockBit/Conti where
